_comment: Reproduction tolerances for the Process-A escape hatch (§5.5, hardened in v5.6). FIXED EX ANTE AND CHECKED IN. These bounds are NEVER chosen or adjusted after seeing a reproduction result — that is the whole point of committing them here. The refit PR reports only pass/fail against these values. Changing this file is itself a decision that must be justified in its own PR, made BEFORE the summer run it will govern.

preference._comment: Bit-identical reproduction is preferred and is the DEFAULT EXPECTATION. The bounds below are a fallback for genuine numerical noise, not a routine allowance.

parameterClasses._comment: One absolute and one relative tolerance per class. A parameter passes if it is within EITHER — absolute alone would be meaningless across scales that differ by two orders of magnitude, relative alone would be meaningless for values near zero.

goalRate.description: Scoring rates, order 3 goals. BASE_TOTAL and its league deltas.

eloScale.description: Elo points per goal, order 200. ELO_PER_GOAL and its league delta.

eloOffset.description: Additive Elo offsets, order 70 but legitimately near zero for the deltas.

structural.description: Not fitted — grid size, extra-time factor, rating noise. These are DECISIONS and must reproduce exactly; any difference is a procedure change, not numerical noise.

comparativeGates._comment: Process B ONLY (§5.5). There is no comparative gate in Process A — in a normal year the incumbent and a fresh fit on the same window arise from identical data and identical code, so comparing them tests nothing.

rollingOrigin._comment: The lab's time-forward protocol: each of the last N completed seasons serves once as hold-out, both procedures fitted on the 15 seasons preceding that fold.

windowProvenance._comment: 15 seasons is the window on which the lab's recency conclusion ('equal weighting beats every half-life') was established. IF THE WINDOW RULE EVER CHANGES, THAT IS A PROCESS B CHANGE and the recency test must be re-run first — otherwise older football is imported into a current forecast.
